description: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.

subcriteria.name: firewall_boundary
subcriteria.reference_text: Firewall rules or security group configurations showing restricted ingress and egress ports. Only necessary ports open such as 443 for HTTPS and 22 for SSH from approved IPs. Default deny rule in place. AWS security groups, GCP firewall rules, or Azure NSGs configured.

subcriteria.name: network_architecture
subcriteria.reference_text: Network architecture diagram showing VPCs, subnets, security groups, load balancers, and network boundaries. DMZ or public-facing tier separated from private subnets. WAF or DDoS protection configured. Network access control lists restricting traffic flow.

subcriteria.name: remote_access
subcriteria.reference_text: Remote access to production systems requires VPN with multi-factor authentication. Direct SSH or RDP access to production instances restricted or disabled. Bastion host or jump box used for administrative access.

subcriteria.name: intrusion_detection
subcriteria.reference_text: Intrusion detection or prevention system monitoring network traffic. IDS/IPS alerts configured for suspicious activity including port scanning, brute force, and lateral movement. GuardDuty, Crowdstrike, or equivalent providing continuous network monitoring.

mismatch_signals.reason: Physical access controls are CC6.4, not network boundary protection.
User lifecycle management is CC6.2/CC6.3, not boundary protection.
